Abstract

with previous research, which has also observed signifi-
cantly longer message reception delays for GPS compared
to BDS3 within B2b corrections (Yu et al. 2022). The loss
of correction data is primarily attributed to bandwidth con-
straints and the higher priority given to BDS3 corrections
over GPS, resulting in the occasional loss of GPS correc-
tion data. Statistical analysis further reveals that on aver-
age, there are approximately 10 BDS3 satellites available per
epoch, compared to 7 for GPS. In extreme cases, the number
of available GPS satellit

validation (Verhagen and Teunissen 2013). More specific
processing flowcharts and strategies are provided in Fig. 2
and detailed further in Table 2.
Results
In this section, the evaluation of PPP-B2b products is ini-
tially presented. Next, the estimated phase OSB products
are examined, with a focus on their time-varying charac-
teristics and the users' integer property performance after
OSB calibration. Finally, positioning tests are carried out to
validate the PPP-IAR performance, in comparison with PPP
ambiguity-float solutions.

by using data from 2 to 24 h each day, reveals that the accu-
racy of the C positioning mode is 4.5 cm in the east, 2.6 cm
in the north, and 9.4 cm in the up direction. Following the
integration of phase OSB products, the positioning errors
in the horizontal components stabilize, maintaining centim-
eter-level accuracy during nearly all periods. Statistically,
the accuracy after convergence in the E/N/U directions is
1.7/1.1/5.9 cm, respectively, representing a 62.2/57.7/37.2%
improvement over float solutions. In CG positioning mode,
